mainTopic: (continues) המוכר שדה לחבירו ונמצאת שאינה שלו
overview: An extensive debate between Rav and Shmuel regarding whether a buyer of land that turns out not to belong to the seller retains the improvements (שבח) and the principal (מעות), including various scenarios of theft, debt, and the nature of the payment. — discussion continues into next amud.

sugyaBoundaries.topic: (continues) המוכר שדה לחבירו ונמצאת שאינה שלו
sugyaBoundaries.gist: An extensive debate between Rav and Shmuel regarding whether a buyer of land that turns out not to belong to the seller retains the improvements (שבח) and the principal (מעות), including various scenarios of theft, debt, and the nature of the payment. — discussion continues into next amud.

steps.title: The Distinction Between Legal and Forceful Appropriation
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara distinguishes between Rava's and Rabba bar Rav Huna's explanations, noting that Rava's explanation implies legal appropriation, while Rabba bar Rav Huna's implies forceful appropriation.
steps.whatsHappening: The גְּמָרָא distinguishes between the two previous explanations. It notes that רַבָּא's case implies בְּדִין מַשְׁמַע, while רַבָּה בַּר רַב הוּנָא's implies בְּעִנָּא מַשְׁמַע.
steps.deeperAnalysis: This linguistic distinction clarifies the legal status of the land transfer. One case involves a legal transfer, while the other involves force.

phrases.aramaic: רָבָא לָא אָמַר כְּרַבָּה בַּר רַב הוּנָא:
phrases.english: Rava did not say like Rabba bar Rav Huna:

phrases.aramaic: ״הֲרֵי הִיא יוֹצְאָה מִתַּחַת יָדוֹ״ – בְּדִינָא מַשְׁמַע.
phrases.english: "It is appropriated from his possession" – this indicates a legal matter.

phrases.aramaic: וְרַבָּה בַּר רַב הוּנָא לָא אָמַר כְּרָבָא:
phrases.english: And Rabba bar Rav Huna did not say like Rava:

phrases.aramaic: ״הֲרֵי הִיא יוֹצְאָה מִתַּחַת יָדוֹ״ – בְּעֵינָא מַשְׁמַע.
phrases.english: "It is appropriated from his possession" – this indicates an unadulterated matter.

steps.triggerLanguage: רָבָא לָא אָמַר כְּרַבָּה בַּר רַב הוּנָא: ״הֲרֵי הִיא יוֹצְאָה מִתַּחַת יָדוֹ״ – בְּדִינָא מַשְׁמַע. וְרַבָּה בַּר רַב הוּנָא לָא אָמַר כְּרָבָא: ״הֲרֵי הִיא יוֹצְאָה מִתַּחַת יָדוֹ״ – בְּעֵינָא מַשְׁמַע.

rashi.hebrew: בדינא משמע - הלכך בהוצאת נגזל קאמר וגובה את הקרן דקאמר כשחפר בו בורות:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that רבא's phrase 'הרי היא יוצאת מתחת ידו' implies legal transfer (בדינא), meaning the land is returned to the original owner and he can collect the principal value of the improvements (the wells) he made.

rashi.hebrew: בעינא משמע - כמו שהיתה ולא שחפר בה בורות:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that רבה בר רב הונא's phrase 'הרי היא יוצאת מתחת ידו' implies forceful removal (בעינא), meaning the land is returned to the original owner in its original state, without compensation for the wells dug by the possessor.

steps.title: רַב אַשֵּׁי's Disjunctive Explanation
steps.stepSummary: Rav Ashi explains that the Baraita teaches its rulings disjunctively, referring to two different cases: one where the buyer collects the principal and one where the robbery victim collects the produce.
steps.whatsHappening: רַב אַשֵּׁי offers a resolution using לְצַדָּדִין קָתְנֵי. He argues the baraita refers to two separate cases.
steps.deeperAnalysis: In one case, the buyer collects the principal. In the other, the נִגְזָל collects the produce. This avoids combining both claims in a single scenario.

phrases.english: Rav Ashi said: It is stated disjunctively,

phrases.aramaic: כְּגוֹן שֶׁגָּזַל שָׂדֶה מֵחֲבֵירוֹ מְלֵאָה פֵּירוֹת
phrases.english: such as one who robbed a field from his fellow full of produce

phrases.aramaic: וְאָכַל אֶת הַפֵּירוֹת וּמָכַר אֶת הַשָּׂדֶה,
phrases.english: and he ate the produce and sold the field,
the purchaser came to collect the principal –
he collects from liened property.
the robbed one came to collect the produce –
he collects from free property.

steps.triggerLanguage: רַב אָשֵׁי אָמַר: לִצְדָדִין קָתָנֵי, כְּגוֹן שֶׁגָּזַל שָׂדֶה מֵחֲבֵירוֹ מְלֵאָה פֵּירוֹת וְאָכַל אֶת הַפֵּירוֹת וּמָכַר אֶת הַשָּׂדֶה, בָּא לוֹקֵחַ לִגְבּוֹת קֶרֶן – גּוֹבֶה מִנְּכָסִים מְשׁוּעְבָּדִים. בָּא נִגְזָל לִגְבּוֹת פֵּירוֹת – גּוֹבֶה מִנְּכָסִים בְּנֵי חוֹרִין.

rashi.hebrew: ומכר את השדה - והרי היא יוצאה מתחת ידו של לוקח בדינא ובעינא:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that when the robber sells the field, the field is legally expropriated from the buyer's possession, necessitating the buyer's claim for the principal.

steps.title: Challenge: Oral Loans Cannot Collect from Liened Property
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara challenges the previous ruling by noting that according to both Rava and Rabba bar Rav Huna, the money owed by a robber to the victim is considered an oral loan, which cannot be collected from liened property.
steps.whatsHappening: The גְּמָרָא raises a difficulty against the previous conclusions. It notes that a גַּזְלָן's debt is a מִלְוָה עַל פֶּה.
steps.deeperAnalysis: According to רַבָּא and רַבָּה בַּר רַב הוּנָא, an oral loan cannot be collected from נְכָסִים מְשׁוּעְבָּדִים. This contradicts the earlier ruling that the victim can collect from liened property.

phrases.aramaic: בֵּין לְרָבָא בֵּין לְרַבָּה בַּר רַב הוּנָא,
phrases.english: Whether to Rava, whether to Rava bar Rav Huna,
It is a loan by word of mouth,

phrases.aramaic: וּמִלְוָה עַל פֶּה אֵינוֹ גּוֹבֶה מִנְּכָסִים מְשׁוּעְבָּדִים!
phrases.english: And a loan by word of mouth does not collect from liened property!

steps.triggerLanguage: בֵּין לְרָבָא בֵּין לְרַבָּה בַּר רַב הוּנָא, מִלְוָה עַל פֶּה הוּא, וּמִלְוָה עַל פֶּה אֵינוֹ גּוֹבֶה מִנְּכָסִים מְשׁוּעְבָּדִים!

rashi.hebrew: בין לרבה בין לרבא - דמוקמי גביית קרן מן הגזלן בנגזל הא מלוה על פה הוא ולא טרפא ממשעבדי:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the Gemara's challenge applies to both רבה and רבא because the victim's claim against the robber is a מלוה על פה, which neither of them permits collecting from נכסים משועבדים.

steps.title: Resolution: The Robber Stood Trial Before Selling
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara resolves the difficulty by specifying that the case involves a robber who stood trial and was found guilty before selling the land, making the debt equivalent to a written loan.
steps.whatsHappening: The גְּמָרָא resolves the difficulty by narrowing the case. It specifies that the גַּזְלָן עָמַד בַּדִּין before selling the land.
steps.deeperAnalysis: Standing trial elevates the debt's status. It becomes equivalent to a written loan, allowing collection from נְכָסִים מְשׁוּעְבָּדִים.

phrases.aramaic: הָכָא בְּמַאי עָסְקִינַן – כְּשֶׁעָמַד בַּדִּין וַהֲדַר זַבֵּין.
phrases.english: Here, with what are we dealing? — When he stood in judgment and then sold.

steps.triggerLanguage: הָכָא בְּמַאי עָסְקִינַן – כְּשֶׁעָמַד בַּדִּין וַהֲדַר זַבֵּן.

rashi.hebrew: כשעמד בדין - קודם שמכר נכסיו וכיון דחייבוהו ב"ד אית ליה קלא והוה ליה כמלוה בשטר:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that when the robber עמד בדין before selling, the court's ruling gives the debt public notice (קלא), making it equivalent to a מלוה בשטר.

steps.title: Challenge: Produce Should Also Be Collectible from Liened Property
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara challenges the resolution by asking why the owner cannot also collect the produce from liened property if the robber stood trial.
steps.whatsHappening: The גְּמָרָא raises a new difficulty. If the גַּזְלָן stood trial, why can't the פֵּירוֹת be collected from נְכָסִים מְשׁוּעְבָּדִים?
steps.deeperAnalysis: The גְּמָרָא answers that the trial was only for the קַרְנָא, not the produce. It adds that סְתָמָא דְּמִלְּתָא is to claim the principal first.

phrases.english: If so, produce as well!
When he stood trial on the principal,

phrases.aramaic: וְלֹא עָמַד בַּדִּין עַל הַפֵּירוֹת. וּמַאי פַּסְקָא?
phrases.english: And did not stand trial on the produce. And what did he rule?
The unstated assumption is that when a person claims –
He claims the principal first.

steps.triggerLanguage: אִי הָכִי, פֵּירוֹת נָמֵי! כְּשֶׁעָמַד בַּדִּין עַל הַקֶּרֶן, וְלֹא עָמַד בַּדִּין עַל הַפֵּירוֹת. וּמַאי פַּסְקָא? סְתָמָא דְּמִלְּתָא כִּי תָּבַע אִינִישׁ – קַרְנָא תָּבַע בְּרֵישָׁא.

rashi.hebrew: מאי פסקא - בתמיה פסקא תנא למלתיה דהעומד בדין אינו עומד על הפירות:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the Gemara's question 'ומאי פסקא' is asking why the תנא stated the law without qualification, given that one who stands trial for the principal does not stand trial for the produce.

tosafot.hebrew: כשעמד בדין על הקרן ולא על הפירות - תימה אם כן מאי מפני תיקון העולם הלא בדין לא גבי דמלוה על פה לא גבי ממשעבדי ויש לומר דאי לאו מפני תיקון העולם הוה אמינא הואיל ועמד בדין על הקרן יש קול גם לפירות:
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות questions why the concept of תיקון העולם is needed here, since a loan on a promissory note (מלוה על פה) generally cannot be collected from liened property anyway, and answers that without תיקון העולם, one might think that standing trial for the principal creates a publicized claim (קול) that extends to the produce as well.

steps.title: Challenge: Shmuel's Statement About a Buyer from a Robber
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara questions Shmuel's statement that a buyer from a robber does not have the right to the value of the enhancement of the land.
steps.whatsHappening: The גְּמָרָא raises a difficulty against שְׁמוּאֵל's statement. It questions if a לֹקֵחַ מִגַּזְלָן truly has no right to the שֶׁבַח.
steps.deeperAnalysis: This challenges the consistency of שְׁמוּאֵל's rulings. It sets up the need to reconcile his statements regarding the buyer's rights.

phrases.aramaic: וְסָבַר שְׁמוּאֵל לוֹקֵחַ מִגַּזְלָן לֵית לֵיהּ שְׁבָחָא?
phrases.english: And did Shmuel think that a buyer from a thief has no enhancement?

steps.triggerLanguage: וְסָבַר שְׁמוּאֵל לוֹקֵחַ מִגַּזְלָן לֵית לֵיהּ שְׁבָחָא?

steps.title: Proof from שְׁמוּאֵל's instruction
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara cites Shmuel's instruction to Rav Hinnana bar Sheilat to write in a deed of sale that the seller commits to compensate the buyer for the value of the enhancement and produce.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא brings a ראיה against שְׁמוּאֵל's earlier statement. It cites his instruction to רַב חִינָנָא בַּר שִׁילַת to write in a deed that the seller compensates for שׁוּפְרָא שְׁבָחָא וּפֵירֵי.
steps.deeperAnalysis: This instruction implies שְׁמוּאֵל holds a buyer from a גַּזְלָן does have rights to the value of enhancements. The proof directly contradicts his previous ruling that such a buyer loses everything.

phrases.aramaic: וְהָא אֲמַר לֵיהּ שְׁמוּאֵל לְרַב חִינָּנָא בַּר שִׁילַת:
phrases.english: And yet Shmuel said to Rav Hinnana bar Shilat:
Write, and it is written: "superior-quality, enhancement, and produce."

steps.triggerLanguage: וְהָא אֲמַר לֵיהּ שְׁמוּאֵל לְרַב חִינָנָא בַּר שִׁילַת: אִמְלֵיךְ, וּכְתוֹב ״שׁוּפְרָא שְׁבָחָא וּפֵירֵי״.

rashi.hebrew: רב חיננא בר שילת - סופר היה:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י identifies רב חיננא בר שילת as a סופר, explaining why שמואל instructed him to write specific terms in the deed.

rashi.hebrew: אמליך - כשאתה כותב שטר המלך במוכר אם יקבל עליו להגבות ללוקח משפר נכסיו קרנא ושבחא ופרי אם יטרפוה ממנו:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that אמליך refers to writing a שטר המלך where the seller accepts the obligation to pay the buyer for קרנא, שבחא, and פרי if the property is repossessed.

steps.title: Resolution: Creditor vs. buyer from גַּזְלָן
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara resolves the difficulty by specifying that the case is a buyer from a robber, not a creditor.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא resolves the contradiction by distinguishing the claimant. The case of the deed involves a בַּעַל חוֹב, not a לוֹקֵחַ מִגַּזְלָן.
steps.deeperAnalysis: שְׁמוּאֵל holds a creditor collects the value of enhancements, but a buyer from a robber does not. This distinction resolves the apparent contradiction in his rulings.

phrases.english: In what?

phrases.aramaic: אִי בְּבַעַל חוֹב – מִי אִית לֵיהּ פֵּירֵי?
phrases.english: If in a creditor — does he have produce?

phrases.aramaic: וְהָאָמַר שְׁמוּאֵל: בַּעַל חוֹב גּוֹבֶה אֶת הַשֶּׁבַח,
phrases.english: And Shmuel said: A creditor collects the enhancement,
Enhancement — yes,
But produce — no.
Rather, is it not in a buyer from a robber!

steps.triggerLanguage: בְּמַאי? אִי בְּבַעַל חוֹב – מִי אִית לֵיהּ פֵּירֵי? וְהָא אָמַר שְׁמוּאֵל: בַּעַל חוֹב גּוֹבֶה אֶת הַשֶּׁבַח, שֶׁבַח – אִין, אֲבָל פֵּירוֹת – לָא. אֶלָּא לָאו, בְּלוֹקֵחַ מִגַּזְלָן!

steps.title: רַב יוֹסֵף's resolution: גַּזְלָן owns land
steps.stepSummary: Rav Yosef resolves the difficulty by specifying that the case is when the robber owns land, which he can return to the buyer instead of paying him money.
steps.whatsHappening: רַב יוֹסֵף offers a different resolution to the difficulty. He states the case involves a גַּזְלָן who owns קַרְקַע to return to the buyer.
steps.deeperAnalysis: If the robber has land, he can return it instead of paying money. This makes the transaction look like a sale rather than a loan with interest, avoiding the prohibition of רִבִּית.

phrases.english: Rav Yosef said:
Here, with what are we dealing?
Such as that he has land.

steps.triggerLanguage: אָמַר רַב יוֹסֵף: הָכָא בְּמַאי עָסְקִינַן – כְּגוֹן שֶׁיֵּשׁ לוֹ קַרְקַע.

rashi.hebrew: כגון שיש לו קרקע - לגזלן זה שמגבהו ללוקח קרקע ולא מעות דהשתא לא מחזי כרבית:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the robber returns land to the buyer rather than money, which avoids the appearance of רבית.

steps.title: Challenge: סְאָה בִּסְאָה prohibited even with land
steps.stepSummary: Abaye challenges Rav Yosef's resolution by asking if it is permitted to borrow a se'a of grain for return of a se'a in a case where the borrower owns land.
steps.whatsHappening: אַבָּיֵי raises a קושיא against רַב יוֹסֵף's resolution. He asks if borrowing סְאָה בִּסְאָה is permitted when the borrower owns land.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The Sages prohibit such a loan even if the borrower has land. This challenges רַב יוֹסֵף's claim that owning land removes the appearance of interest.

phrases.english: Abaye said to him:

phrases.aramaic: וְכִי מוּתָּר לִלְוֹת סְאָה בִּסְאָה בִּמְקוֹם
phrases.english: And is it permitted to borrow a se'ah for a se'ah in a place
where he has land?

steps.triggerLanguage: אֲמַר לֵיהּ אַבָּיֵי: וְכִי מוּתָּר לִלְוֹת סְאָה בִּסְאָה בִּמְקוֹם שֶׁיֵּשׁ לוֹ קַרְקַע?

rashi.hebrew: אסור ללות סאה בסאה - שמא יוקרו חטין:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the prohibition against borrowing a סאה for a סאה exists because of the concern that wheat prices might rise, making the loan resemble a loan with interest.

steps.title: Resolution: Distinction between הַלְוָאָה and זְבִינֵי
steps.stepSummary: Rav Yosef resolves the difficulty by distinguishing between a loan and a sale.
steps.whatsHappening: רַב יוֹסֵף resolves the difficulty by distinguishing between a הַלְוָאָה and זְבִינֵי. The prohibition applies to loans, but not to sales.
steps.deeperAnalysis: In a loan, the prohibition stands even if the borrower owns land. In a sale, the additional value paid by the robber does not appear to be interest.

phrases.aramaic: אֲמַר לֵיהּ, הָתָם הַלְוָאָה, הָכָא זְבִינֵי.
phrases.english: He said to him: There, a loan; here, sales.

steps.triggerLanguage: אֲמַר לֵיהּ, הָתָם הַלְוָאָה, הָכָא זְבִינֵי.

rashi.hebrew: בהלואה - שייך רבית טפי ממכר:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that רבית is more applicable to a הלואה than to a מקח וממכר.

steps.title: Alternative resolution: קָנוּ מִיָּדוֹ
steps.stepSummary: Some say that Rav Yosef's resolution is that the buyer performed an act of acquisition at the time of purchase, formalizing a condition that the seller will reimburse for enhancement.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא presents an alternative resolution from רַב יוֹסֵף. The case involves a buyer who performed קָנוּ מִיָּדוֹ at the time of purchase.
steps.deeperAnalysis: This act formalizes a condition that the seller will reimburse for enhancements. It makes the transaction look like a sale, avoiding the appearance of interest.

phrases.english: There are those who say, Rav Yosef said:
Here with what are we dealing –
such as that he acquired from his hand.

steps.triggerLanguage: אִיכָּא דְּאָמְרִי, אָמַר רַב יוֹסֵף: הָכָא בְּמַאי עָסְקִינַן – כְּגוֹן שֶׁקָּנוּ מִיָּדוֹ.

rashi.hebrew: שקנו מידו - על השבח דהוה ליה מחויב משעת המכר קודם שיש שכר המתנת מעות:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the קניין was performed specifically regarding the שבח (enhancement), making the seller obligated from the time of the sale, before the money was actually delayed.

tosafot.hebrew: כגון שקנו מידו - וא"ת א"כ אמאי קאמר אמליך הלא סתם קנין לכתיבה עומד וי"ל ה"מ כגון שהקנה לו דבר שהוא בעין דכיון דיפה כחו לעשות קנין רוצה שיכתבו לו:
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות addresses why the Gemara specifies אמליך (a loan) if a standard קניין is for writing a document, answering that the קניין here was for an item that is בעין (physically present), so the seller wanted a document written to strengthen the buyer's hold.

steps.title: Challenge: סְאָה בִּסְאָה prohibited even with קָנוּ מִיָּדוֹ
steps.stepSummary: Abaye challenges the alternative resolution by asking if it is permitted to borrow a se'a of grain for return of a se'a in a case where the borrower performed an act of acquisition.
steps.whatsHappening: אַבָּיֵי raises a קושיא against the alternative resolution. He asks if borrowing סְאָה בִּסְאָה is permitted when the borrower performed קָנוּ מִיָּדוֹ.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The Sages prohibit such a loan even if the borrower performed an act of acquisition. This challenges the claim that קָנוּ מִיָּדוֹ removes the appearance of interest.

phrases.english: Abaye said to him:

phrases.aramaic: וְכִי מוּתָּר לִלְוֹת סְאָה בִּסְאָה בִּמְקוֹם שֶׁקָּנוּ מִיָּדוֹ?
phrases.english: But is it permitted to borrow a se'a for a se'a in a place where he acquired it from his…

steps.triggerLanguage: אֲמַר לֵיהּ אַבָּיֵי: וְכִי מוּתָּר לִלְוֹת סְאָה בִּסְאָה בִּמְקוֹם שֶׁקָּנוּ מִיָּדוֹ?

steps.title: Resolution: Distinction between הַלְוָאָה and זְבִינֵי
steps.stepSummary: Rav Yosef resolves the difficulty by distinguishing between a loan and a sale.
steps.whatsHappening: רַב יוֹסֵף resolves the difficulty by distinguishing between a הַלְוָאָה and זְבִינֵי. The prohibition applies to loans, but not to sales.
steps.deeperAnalysis: In a loan, the prohibition stands even if the borrower performed קָנוּ מִיָּדוֹ. In a sale, the additional value paid by the robber does not appear to be interest.

phrases.aramaic: אֲמַר לֵיהּ: הָתָם הַלְוָאָה, וְהָכָא זְבִינֵי.
phrases.english: He said to him: There it is a loan, and here it is sales.

steps.triggerLanguage: אֲמַר לֵיהּ: הָתָם הַלְוָאָה, וְהָכָא זְבִינֵי.

steps.title: רָבָא supports שְׁמוּאֵל with deed language
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara returns to discuss Shmuel's statement that a creditor collects the value of the enhancement of the land.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא returns to שְׁמוּאֵל's ruling. רָבָא supports it by citing the standard language in a deed of sale.
steps.deeperAnalysis: רָבָא points to the standard clause in a deed of sale. This clause states the seller accepts responsibility if the land is repossessed by a creditor, including the value of the enhancement. This textual evidence bolsters שְׁמוּאֵל's position.

phrases.aramaic: גּוּפָא, אָמַר שְׁמוּאֵל: בַּעַל חוֹב גּוֹבֶה אֶת הַשֶּׁבַח.
phrases.english: The matter itself, Shmuel said: A creditor collects the enhancement.
Rava said: Know,
that the seller writes to the buyer:

phrases.aramaic: ״אֲנָא אֵיקוּם וַאֲשַׁפֵּי וַאֲדַכֵּי וַאֲמָרֵיק זְבִינֵי אִילֵּין,
phrases.english: "I will stand and silence and purify and cleanse these sales,
they and their labor and their enhancement, and I will stand before you,
and I will give the value of these and accept upon them."

steps.triggerLanguage: גּוּפָא, אָמַר שְׁמוּאֵל: בַּעַל חוֹב גּוֹבֶה אֶת הַשֶּׁבַח. אָמַר רָבָא: תֵּדַע, שֶׁכָּךְ כּוֹתֵב לוֹ מוֹכֵר לַלּוֹקֵחַ: ״אֲנָא אֵיקוּם וַאֲשַׁפֵּי וַאֲדַכֵּי וַאֲמָרֵיק זְבִינֵי אִילֵּין, אִינּוּן וְעַמְלֵיהוֹן וּשְׁבָחֵיהוֹן, וְאֵיקוּם קֳדָמָךְ, וּצְבִי זָבֹינָא דְּנַן וְקַבֵּיל עֲלוֹהִי״.

rashi.hebrew: תדע - דגובה ולא מצי לוקח למימר אנא אשבחי:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that רבא's proof from the deed of sale demonstrates that the creditor collects the enhancement, preventing the buyer from claiming he should pay for the enhancement himself.

rashi.hebrew: שכך כותב לו מוכר ללוקח - באחריות שטר המכר:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that the seller writes to the buyer in the deed of sale that the sale is guaranteed against repossession.

rashi.hebrew: ואשפי - אשקיט ודומה לו על הר נשפה דמתרגם על טורא שליוא (ישעיהו י״ג:ב׳):
rashi.takeaway: רש״י defines the Aramaic term ואשפי as meaning to quiet or silence any claims against the property.

rashi.hebrew: ואדכי - אטהר מכל ערעור:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that ואדכי means to purify the property from all disputes or encumbrances.

rashi.hebrew: אינון ועמליהון ושבחיהון - וכיון דעל מוכר הדר גבי שבחא בעל חוב מלוקח וחוזר הלוקח על המוכר:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that since the seller is responsible for the enhancement, the creditor collects it from the buyer, and the buyer then recovers the cost from the seller.

tosafot.hebrew: בעל חוב גובה את השבח - נראה דגבי אפי' מן השבח שהשביחו יתומים אע"פ שאין להם על מי לחזור והוה ליה כמתנה דאמרינן לקמן דלא גבי שבחא מ"מ גובה מהן משום דכרעיה דאבוהון נינהו כדמוכח בפרק יש בכור (בכורות דף נב. ושם) דתנן אין הבכור נוטל פי שנים בראוי כבמוחזק ולא בשבח ולא האשה בכתובתה ולא הבנות במזונותיהן ופריך בגמרא והאמר שמואל בעל חוב גובה את השבח ומשני מקולי כתובה שנו כאן ומזון בנות נמי תנאי כתובה ככתובה דמי והתם משבח יתומים מיירי דאי משבח לקוחות אם כן מאי אריא דלא טרפי בנות משבח והלא מגוף הקרקע נמי לא טרפי דאין מוציאין למזון האשה והבנות מנכסים משועבדים אלא ודאי בשבח יתומים מיירי ודוקא כתובת אשה ומזונות דקילי לא גבו משבח אבל שאר בע"ח גבי ורש"י פירש התם מזון הבנות כגון נשא אשה ופסק לזון בתה ה' שנים ולפירושו אין ראיה דמצי מיירי בשבח לקוחות אך לא נהירא דלא שייך בהו תנאי כתובה והא דאמר בהמקבל (לקמן בבא מציעא דף קי.) יתומים אומרים אנו השבחנו ואין השבח שלך משמע דלא גבי משבח יתומים התם מיירי כשעשה אפותיקי ואומרים אנו השבחנו ותן לנו היציאה כדין יורד לתוך שדה חבירו שלא ברשות וכן מוקי התם במסקנא כשעשאה אפותיקי:
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות explains that a creditor can collect the enhancement even from orphans who improved the land, because the land is considered their father's property, unlike a wife's כתובה or daughters' מזונות which are lenient and do not collect enhancements from orphans.

tosafot.hebrew: תדע שכך כתב כו' - אין לפרש דהכי מוכח דבע"ח גובה את השבח דבשביל דבע"ח גובה את השבח כותב מוכר ללוקח שישלם לו את השבח דא"כ מאי פריך מתנה דלא כתיב בה הכי נמי דלא טריף שבח והלא אין תלוי גביית הבע"ח בכתיבת המוכר אלא יש לומר מדמשלם מוכר ללוקח את השבח אין לחוש אם יגבהו בעל חוב ממנו דאין הלוקח מפסיד כל כך וא"ת ומאי ראיה היא דלמא מיירי בלוקח מגזלן דנגזל ודאי אית ליה שבחא וי"ל דאי בלוקח מן הגזלן איירי הוה ליה למכתב נמי ופיריהון אלא מיירי בבעל חוב דלית ליה פירי אי נמי מדקאמר ועמליהון היינו הוצאה ושבחיהון היינו השבח היתר על היציאה ואי בנגזל הא נגזל גופיה יהיב ליה יציאה כדין יורד לתוך שדה חבירו שלא ברשות:
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות clarifies that רבא's proof is not that the deed's wording creates the law, but rather that since the seller compensates the buyer for the enhancement, the buyer suffers no loss if the creditor collects it, and the mention of עמליהון and שבחיהון indicates this applies to a creditor rather than a thief who would also be liable for פירות.

steps.title: Challenge from מַתָּנָה
steps.stepSummary: Rav Hiyya bar Avin challenges Rava's statement by asking if a creditor does not repossess the value of the enhancement of the land from the recipient of a gift.
steps.whatsHappening: רַב חִיָּיא בַּר אָבִין challenges רָבָא's proof. He asks why a creditor does not collect the enhancement from a recipient of a gift.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The challenge hinges on the absence of the standard deed clause in a gift document. If the deed clause proves the creditor collects the enhancement, its absence in a gift should mean the creditor does not collect it. Yet, the law is that the creditor does not collect the enhancement from a gift recipient, creating a contradiction.

phrases.aramaic: אֲמַר לֵיהּ רַב חִיָּיא בַּר אָבִין לְרָבָא:
phrases.english: Rav Ḥiyya bar Avin said to Rava:

phrases.aramaic: אֶלָּא מֵעַתָּה מַתָּנָה דְּלָא כָּתֵיב לֵיהּ הָכִי,
phrases.english: But now, a gift where he did not write this to him,
Here too, does he not repossess the enhancement?
He said to him: Yes.

steps.triggerLanguage: אֲמַר לֵיהּ רַב חִיָּיא בַּר אָבִין לְרָבָא: אֶלָּא מֵעַתָּה מַתָּנָה דְּלָא כָּתֵיב לֵיהּ הָכִי, הָכִי נָמֵי דְּלָא טָרֵיף שְׁבָחָא?! אֲמַר לֵיהּ: אִין.

rashi.hebrew: מתנה דלא כתיב ביה הכי - שאין אדם מקבל עליו אחריות מה שהוא נותן:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that a deed of gift lacks the standard clause of אחריות, meaning the giver does not accept responsibility for the property's value.

rashi.hebrew: ה"נ - דאם נותן נכסיו במתנה לא גבי בעל חוב שבחא הואיל ולא הדר על הנותן:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that since the creditor cannot recover the value from the giver in a case of gift, the creditor likewise cannot collect the value of the enhancement from the recipient.

steps.title: Challenge: Gift is stronger than sale
steps.stepSummary: Rav Hiyya bar Avin challenges Rava's statement by asking if the legal power of a gift is stronger than that of a sale.
steps.whatsHappening: רַב חִיָּיא בַּר אָבִין raises a second difficulty. He questions why a gift is considered legally stronger than a sale regarding the enhancement.
steps.deeperAnalysis: In a sale, the buyer loses the enhancement value if the land is repossessed. In a gift, the recipient does not get the enhancement value back from the giver. This implies the gift's legal standing is stronger, which contradicts the assumption that a sale's deed clause dictates the law.

phrases.english: And is the power of a gift better than a sale?
He said to him: Yes, it is better and better.

steps.triggerLanguage: וְכִי יָפֶה כֹּחַ מַתָּנָה מִמֶּכֶר? אֲמַר לֵיהּ: אִין יָפֶה וְיָפֶה.

rashi.hebrew: וכי יפה כח מתנה כו' - בתמיה:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the Gemara's question is asked in a tone of astonishment, implying that it is counterintuitive for a gift to have stronger legal force than a sale.

rashi.hebrew: יפה ויפה - בדבר זה דכיון דלא הדר גבי ליה לא מפסיד ליה במידי דלא אפסדיה לבעל חוב דבשלמא גופה של קרקע א"ל אמאי (זבנתיה) מאין אגבה חובי אבל שבחא א"ל מאי אפסדתיך:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the answer 'יפה ויפה' means that in the case of a gift, the giver does not lose anything because the creditor cannot claim the land itself, and regarding the enhancements, the creditor cannot claim any loss since the giver never received value for them.

steps.title: רַב נַחְמָן brings a בְּרֵיתָא
steps.stepSummary: Rav Naḥman cites a baraita that supports Shmuel's statement that a creditor collects the value of the enhancement of the land.
steps.whatsHappening: רַב נַחְמָן brings a בְּרֵיתָא to support שְׁמוּאֵל. The בְּרֵיתָא discusses a buyer collecting compensation from a seller.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The בְּרֵיתָא states the buyer collects the principal from liened property and the enhancement from unsold property. This aligns with שְׁמוּאֵל's view that the creditor collects the enhancement, as the buyer must seek it from the seller's remaining assets.

phrases.english: Rav Naḥman said:

phrases.aramaic: הָא מַתְנִיתָא מְסַיַּיע לֵיהּ לְמָר שְׁמוּאֵל,
phrases.english: This Mishnah supports Mar Shmuel,

phrases.aramaic: וְהוּנָא חַבְרִין מוֹקֵים לַהּ בְּמִילֵּי אַחֲרִינֵי.
phrases.english: and our colleague Rav Huna establishes it regarding other matters.
As it is taught: One who sells a field to his fellow,
and behold it is going out from under his hand,
phrases.notes: Refers to the field being repossessed by the seller's creditor.

phrases.english: when he collects –
he collects the principal from encumbered property,
and enhancement he collects from free property.

steps.triggerLanguage: אָמַר רַב נַחְמָן: הָא מַתְנִיתָא מְסַיַּע לֵיהּ לְמָר שְׁמוּאֵל, וְהוּנָא חַבְרִין מוֹקֵים לַהּ בְּמִלֵּי אַחֲרִינֵי. דְּתַנְיָא: הַמוֹכֵר שָׂדֶה לַחֲבֵירוֹ, וַהֲרֵי הִיא יוֹצְאָה מִתַּחַת יָדוֹ, כְּשֶׁהוּא גּוֹבֶה – גּוֹבֶה אֶת הַקֶּרֶן מִנְּכָסִים מְשׁוּעְבָּדִים, וְשֶׁבַח גּוֹבֶה מִנְּכָסִים בְּנֵי חוֹרִין.

rashi.hebrew: מסייע ליה למר שמואל - דבעל חוב גובה את השבח:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the baraita supports שמואל's ruling that a creditor collects the שבח (enhancement) of the property.

rashi.hebrew: כשהוא גובה - כשלוקח חוזר וגובה מן המוכר:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that the phrase כשהוא גובה refers to the buyer collecting compensation from the seller.

steps.title: רַב הוּנָא resolves with לוקח מגזלן
steps.stepSummary: Rav Huna interprets the baraita as referring to a buyer from a robber, not a creditor.
steps.whatsHappening: רַב הוּנָא resolves the difficulty by reinterpreting the בְּרֵיתָא. He suggests it refers to a buyer from a robber, not a creditor.
steps.deeperAnalysis: If the בְּרֵיתָא refers to a buyer from a robber, the rule that the buyer collects the enhancement from unsold property makes sense. This is because a buyer from a robber does not have the right to the enhancement from the robber, so he must seek it from the original owner's remaining assets.

phrases.aramaic: וְהוּנָא חַבְרִין מוֹקֵים לַהּ בְּמִילֵּי אַחֲרִינֵי, בְּלוֹקֵחַ מִגַּזְלָן.
phrases.english: And Rav Huna, our colleague, establishes it in other matters, in one who buys from a robber.

steps.triggerLanguage: וְהוּנָא חַבְרִין מוֹקֵים לַהּ בְּמִלֵּי אַחֲרִינֵי, בְּלוֹקֵחַ מִגַּזְלָן.

rashi.hebrew: בלוקח מגזלן - דנגזל ודאי טריף שבחא דאמר ליה ארעאי אשבח אבל בעל חוב כל כמה דלא טרפה לה לאו דידיה הוא וברשותא דלוקח אשבח:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that רב הונא resolves the difficulty by interpreting the ברייתא as referring to a buyer from a robber, because the victim of robbery certainly has the right to collect the enhancement (שבחא) of the land, whereas a creditor does not own the property until it is seized, so the enhancement belongs to the buyer.

steps.title: Proof from another בְּרֵיתָא
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara cites another baraita about a buyer who enhances the land and a creditor repossesses it.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא cites another בְּרֵיתָא about a buyer who enhances the land. It details compensation rules when a creditor repossesses the land.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The בְּרֵיתָa distinguishes between two scenarios. If the enhancement exceeds expenses, the buyer takes the difference from the owner and expenses from the creditor. If expenses exceed enhancement, the buyer only gets compensation up to the enhancement value from the creditor.

phrases.aramaic: תַּנְיָא אִידַּךְ: הַמּוֹכֵר שָׂדֶה לַחֲבֵירוֹ וְהִשְׁבִּיחָהּ,
phrases.english: It is taught in another baraita: One who sells a field to his fellow and…

phrases.aramaic: וּבָא בַּעַל חוֹב וּטְרָפָהּ, כְּשֶׁהוּא גּוֹבֶה,
phrases.english: and a creditor came and repossessed it, when he collects,
if the enhancement is greater than the expenditure –
he takes the enhancement from the owner of the land,
and the expenditure from the creditor.
And if the expenditure is greater than the enhancement –

phrases.aramaic: אֵין לוֹ אֶלָּא הוֹצָאָה שִׁיעוּר שֶׁבַח מִבַּעַל חוֹב.
phrases.english: he has only expenditure equal to the enhancement from the creditor.

steps.triggerLanguage: תַּנְיָא אִידַךְ: הַמוֹכֵר שָׂדֶה לַחֲבֵירוֹ וְהִשְׁבִּיחָהּ, וּבָא בַּעַל חוֹב וּטְרָפָהּ, כְּשֶׁהוּא גּוֹבֶה, אִם הַשֶּׁבַח יוֹתֵר עַל הַיְצִיאָה – נוֹטֵל אֶת הַשֶּׁבַח מִבַּעַל הַקַּרְקַע, וְהַיְצִיאָה מִבַּעַל חוֹב. וְאִם הַיְצִיאָה יְתֵירָה עַל הַשֶּׁבַח – אֵין לוֹ אֶלָּא הוֹצָאָה שִׁעוּר שֶׁבַח מִבַּעַל חוֹב.

rashi.hebrew: על הוצאה - שהוציא הלוקח בשבחה:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that הוצאה refers to the expenses the buyer incurred to enhance the land.

rashi.hebrew: נוטל - הלוקח:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that נוטל refers to the buyer.

rashi.hebrew: את השבח מבעל הקרקע - מה שעודף על היציאה דאמר ליה פרעתי את חובך:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the buyer takes the excess enhancement value from the landowner because he can claim he paid off the debt with the land's value.

rashi.hebrew: אין לו - ללוקח אלא מן היציאה שהוציא בה פורע לו שיעור שבח והשאר מפסיד:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that when expenses exceed the enhancement value, the buyer only recovers from the creditor up to the amount of the enhancement, losing the remainder of his expenses.

steps.title: Challenge to שְׁמוּאֵל's view
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara asks how Shmuel interprets the baraita, and raises difficulties against both possible interpretations.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא challenges how שְׁמוּאֵל interprets this בְּרֵיתָא. It questions whether the בְּרֵיתָא refers to a buyer from a robber or a creditor.
steps.deeperAnalysis: If it refers to a buyer from a robber, the first clause contradicts שְׁמוּאֵל, who says such a buyer has no right to the enhancement. If it refers to a creditor, both clauses contradict שְׁמוּאֵל, who says a creditor collects the enhancement and pays nothing.

phrases.english: And how does Shmuel establish it?
If in one who buys from a robber –
The beginning is difficult, as Shmuel says:
One who buys from a robber has no enhancement.
If in a creditor –
The beginning and the end are difficult, as Shmuel says:
A creditor collects the enhancement!

steps.triggerLanguage: וְהָא שְׁמוּאֵל בְּמַאי מוֹקֵים לַהּ? אִי בְּלוֹקֵחַ מִגַּזְלָן – קַשְׁיָא רֵישָׁא, דְּאָמַר שְׁמוּאֵל: לוֹקֵחַ מִגַּזְלָן לֵית לֵיהּ שְׁבָחָא. אִי בְּבַעַל חוֹב – קַשְׁיָא רֵישָׁא וְסֵיפָא, דְּאָמַר שְׁמוּאֵל: בַּעַל חוֹב גּוֹבֶה אֶת הַשֶּׁבַח!

rashi.hebrew: אי בלוקח מגזלן - האי בע"ח היינו נגזל:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the term בעל חוב in the baraita refers to the נגזל, the original owner whose property was stolen.

rashi.hebrew: קשיא רישא - דקתני נוטל את השבח מבעל הקרקע:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that the difficulty with the first clause is that it states the buyer collects the enhancement from the landowner, contradicting שמואל's ruling that a buyer from a robber has no right to the enhancement.

rashi.hebrew: קשיא רישא וסיפא - דקתני בע"ח נותן את היציאה ולשמואל בעל חוב גובה השבח ואינו נותן כלום:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the difficulty with both clauses when referring to a creditor is that the baraita states the creditor must pay for the enhancement, whereas שמואל rules that a creditor collects the enhancement without paying anything.

steps.title: Alternative resolutions
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara offers two alternative resolutions to the previous difficulty regarding interest: either the case involves a robber who owns other land to compensate, or the buyer acquired the enhancement at the time of purchase.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא offers two alternative resolutions to a prior difficulty. These address whether a creditor collecting enhancements constitutes interest.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The first resolution suggests the robber has other land to compensate the buyer, avoiding interest issues. The second suggests the buyer performed a קניין on the enhancement at purchase, making it a valid condition rather than interest.

phrases.aramaic: אִיבָּעֵית אֵימָא בְּלוֹקֵחַ מִגַּזְלָן, כְּגוֹן שֶׁיֵּשׁ לוֹ קַרְקַע.
phrases.english: If you wish, say regarding one who buys from a robber, such as that he has land.
Or even regarding one who acquires from his hand.

steps.triggerLanguage: אִיבָּעֵית אֵימָא בְּלוֹקֵחַ מִגַּזְלָן

steps.title: Resolution: Creditor and Types of Enhancement
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara offers another resolution: the case involves a creditor, distinguishing between enhancements that reach the shoulders and those that do not.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא offers a third תירוץ involving a בַּעַל חוֹב. It distinguishes between two categories of שֶׁבַח to resolve the קושיא.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The distinction separates שֶׁבַח הַמַּגִּיעַ לַכְּתֵפַיִם, which is nearly independent, from שֶׁבַח שֶׁאֵינוֹ מַגִּיעַ לַכְּתֵפַיִם, which remains attached to the קרקע. This classification clarifies שמואל's ruling by limiting the creditor's rights based on the enhancement's status.

phrases.aramaic: אִיבָּעֵית אֵימָא בְּבַעַל חוֹב. וְלָא קַשְׁיָא:
phrases.english: If you wish, say: regarding a creditor. And it is not difficult:
Here: regarding enhancement that reaches the shoulders,
Here: regarding enhancement that does not reach the shoulders.

steps.triggerLanguage: אִיבָּעֵית אֵימָא בְּבַעַל חוֹב
